pg80-p1-s1: Before taking drastic action which might implicate other people we secure their consent.
pg80-p1-s2:
If we have obtained permission,
have consulted with others, asked God to help and the drastic step is indicated we must not shrink.
pg80-p1:
Before taking drastic action which might implicate other people we secure their consent. If we have obtained permission,
have consulted with others, asked God to help and the drastic step is indicated we must not shrink.
pg80-p2-s1: This brings to mind a story about one of our friends.
pg80-p2-s2:
While drinking, he accepted a sum of money from a bitterly-hated
business rival, giving him no receipt for it.
pg80-p2-s3:
He subsequently denied having received the money and used the incident as
a basis for discrediting the man.
pg80-p2-s4: He thus used his own wrong-doing as a means of destroying the reputation of another.
pg80-p2-s5: In fact, his rival was ruined.
pg80-p2:
This brings to mind a story about one of our friends. While drinking, he accepted a sum of money from a bitterly-hated
business rival, giving him no receipt for it. He subsequently denied having received the money and used the incident as
a basis for discrediting the man. He thus used his own wrong-doing as a means of destroying the reputation of another. In fact, his rival was ruined.
pg80-p3-s1: He felt that he had done a wrong he could not possibly make right.
pg80-p3-s2:
If he opened that old affair, he was afraid it would
destroy the reputation of his partner, disgrace his family and take away his means of livelihood.
pg80-p3-s3:
What right had he to
involve those dependent upon him?
pg80-p3-s4: How could he possibly make a public statement exonerating his rival?
pg80-p3:
He felt that he had done a wrong he could not possibly make right. If he opened that old affair, he was afraid it would
destroy the reputation of his partner, disgrace his family and take away his means of livelihood. What right had he to
involve those dependent upon him? How could he possibly make a public statement exonerating his rival?
pg80-p4-s1:
After consulting with his wife and partner he came to the conclusion that is was better to take those risks than to
stand before his Creator guilty of such ruinous slander.
pg80-p4-s2:
He saw that he had to place the outcome in God's hands or he
would soon start drinking again, and all would be lost anyhow.
pg80-p4-s3: He attended church for the first time in many years.
pg80-p4-s4: After the sermon, he quietly got up and made an explanation.
pg80-p4-s5:
His action met wide-spread approval, and today he is one of
the most trusted citizens of his town.
pg80-p4:
After consulting with his wife and partner he came to the conclusion that is was better to take those risks than to
stand before his Creator guilty of such ruinous slander. He saw that he had to place the outcome in God's hands or he
would soon start drinking again, and all would be lost anyhow. He attended church for the first time in many years. After the sermon, he quietly got up and made an explanation. His action met wide-spread approval, and today he is one of
the most trusted citizens of his town. This all happened years ago.
pg80-p5-s1: The chances are that we have domestic troubles.
pg80-p5-s2: Perhaps we are mixed up with women in a fashion we
